quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungary is home to one of the oldest metro systems in the world! The Budapest Metro Line 1, which opened in 1896, was the first electric underground railway in continental Europe and is still running today. It was built to celebrate Hungary's 1000th anniversary and is now a UNESCO World Heritage site.

short_read.title: The Secret Language of Trees
short_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that trees communicate with each other through an underground network of fungi, sharing nutrients and even warning each other of danger. It's like a 'wood wide web' right beneath our feet!

medium_read.title: Return of the Sea Otters to the Pacific Northwest
medium_read.teaser: After nearly disappearing from the Pacific coast, sea otters are making a remarkable comeback in the waters around the Olympic Peninsula. Their return is transforming entire marine ecosystems in wonderful ways.

long_read.title: The Women Who Mapped the Ocean Floor
long_read.teaser: In the 1950s, Marie Tharp created the first detailed maps of the ocean floor, revealing underwater mountain ranges and transforming our understanding of Earth. Her story is one of persistence, curiosity, and scientific breakthrough.

port_angeles.title: Hurricane Ridge Wildflower Season
port_angeles.description: July is peak wildflower season at Hurricane Ridge! The alpine meadows are bursting with color right now—purple lupines, red paintbrush, and white avalanche lilies carpet the mountainsides. Black-tailed deer often graze among the flowers, and on clear days you can see across the Strait to Canada.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right! Amazing, isn't it? Their tiny hearts work incredibly hard to power those rapid wings!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1200 beats per minute—that's about 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The answer is 1200 beats per minute! It's hard to imagine such a tiny heart beating that fast, but it takes incredible energy to hover in mid-air.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the fastest metabolism of any animal on Earth. To fuel their high-energy lifestyle, they must eat about half their body weight in nectar every day and visit hundreds of flowers.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'hello' or 'goodbye'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Szia! You got it! It's such a friendly, versatile word used throughout the day.
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is 'Szia'! It's one of the most commonly used Hungarian greetings—casual and warm, perfect for friends and family.
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is part of the Finno-Ugric language family, making it quite different from other European languages. It's related to Finnish and Estonian but uses many unique expressions and has a beautifully logical structure.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree is the tallest species in the world?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes, the Coast Redwood! Some of these magnificent trees in California reach over 380 feet tall—taller than the Statue of Liberty!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the Coast Redwood! These incredible trees grow along the California coast and can live for over 2,000 years. The tallest one discovered, named Hyperion, stands 380 feet tall.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Coast Redwoods thrive in the foggy coastal climate of Northern California. The fog provides nearly 40% of their water needs during the dry summer months, condensing on their needles and dripping to the roots.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
